rationale: Both correctly bisect the failure to PR #4834's intentional level-2 wake guard (matching yenuo26's thread comment) and propose the same level=1 test fix, but both overreach by confidently declaring the issue already fixed and closeable, when the actual thread shows Flink-ddd still requesting a full CI verification run rather than confirming resolution. Y is slightly more thorough and better grounded, citing additional guard locations (sleep() flag-setting, the generate() partial-wake RuntimeError) and adding practical runtime guidance for users choosing between level=1/level=2, giving it an edge on completeness and grounding despite near-identical (and similarly overconfident) correctness to X.
